title: Ο περί Ηλεκτρονικής Ταυτοποίησης και Υπηρεσιών Εμπιστοσύνης για Ηλεκτρονικές Συναλλαγές Νόμος
title_en: Electronic Identification and Trust Services for Electronic Transactions Law
description: This Law implements Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 (eIDAS) in the Republic of Cyprus and establishes the national framework for electronic identification and trust services for electronic transactions. It designates the competent supervisory body for trust service providers, sets out conformity assessment procedures, and establishes requirements for the acceptance of electronic identification means. The Law ensures legal certainty and interoperability for electronic signatures, seals, time stamps, and registered delivery services within Cyprus and across the European Union.

provisions.title: Article 1. Subject Matter and Scope
provisions.content: This Law establishes the national framework for the implementation of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council on electronic identification and trust services for electronic transactions in the internal market. It designates the competent authority responsible for supervising qualified trust service providers established in the Republic of Cyprus, specifies procedures for the notification of national electronic identification schemes to the European Commission, and establishes requirements applicable to public sector bodies concerning the acceptance of electronic identification means and trust services. The Law applies to electronic identification schemes notified by the Republic of Cyprus and to trust service providers established within its territory.

provisions.title: Article 2. Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law: 'electronic identification' means the process of using person identification data in electronic form uniquely representing either a natural or legal person or a natural person representing a legal person; 'electronic identification scheme' means a system for electronic identification under which electronic identification means are issued to natural or legal persons or natural persons representing legal persons; 'trust service' means an electronic service normally provided for remuneration which consists of the creation, verification, and validation of electronic signatures, electronic seals, electronic time stamps, electronic registered delivery services, website authentication, or the creation, verification and validation of certificates used for these services; 'trust service provider' means a natural or a legal person who provides one or more trust services either as a qualified or as a non-qualified trust service provider.

provisions.title: Article 3. Competent Supervisory Authority
provisions.content: The Department of Electronic Communications, operating under the supervision of the Minister responsible for digital affairs, is designated as the supervisory body responsible for the supervision of trust service providers established in the Republic of Cyprus, in accordance with the requirements of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The supervisory body shall establish and maintain a trusted list of qualified trust service providers and the qualified trust services they provide, in accordance with the formats and procedures specified by the European Commission. The supervisory body shall cooperate with supervisory bodies of other Member States and with the European Commission in matters relating to cross-border supervision and the interoperability of trust services.

provisions.title: Article 4. Electronic Identification Schemes
provisions.content: The Republic of Cyprus may notify electronic identification schemes to the European Commission in accordance with Article 9 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The Minister responsible for digital affairs shall be responsible for managing the notification process and for ensuring that notified schemes comply with the requirements of the Regulation. Public sector bodies shall accept electronic identification means issued under electronic identification schemes notified by other Member States at the assurance level concerned and, where required under Union or national law, at higher assurance levels. The supervisory body shall establish technical and organisational requirements for assurance levels applicable to national electronic identification schemes.

provisions.title: Article 5. Qualified Trust Service Providers
provisions.content: Trust service providers seeking qualified status shall submit an application to the supervisory body accompanied by a conformity assessment report issued by a conformity assessment body. The supervisory body shall review the application and, if the requirements of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 are satisfied, shall grant qualified status and include the trust service provider on the trusted list within a specified timeframe. Qualified trust service providers shall undergo a conformity assessment at least every twenty-four months and shall notify the supervisory body of any changes to their qualified trust services without undue delay. The supervisory body may suspend or withdraw qualified status where a provider no longer satisfies the applicable requirements.

provisions.title: Article 6. Conformity Assessment Bodies
provisions.content: Conformity assessment bodies accredited in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 765/2008 by the Cyprus Organisation for Standardisation and Control of Quality or by an accreditation body of another Member State may carry out conformity assessments of trust service providers for the purposes of this Law. The supervisory body shall maintain a list of recognised conformity assessment bodies and shall verify the competence and impartiality of bodies included on that list. Conformity assessment reports shall be prepared in accordance with standards specified by the European Commission and shall cover all aspects of the trust service provider's operations relevant to qualified status, including security management, key management, and certificate issuance procedures.

provisions.title: Article 7. Electronic Signatures
provisions.content: An electronic signature shall not be denied legal effect and admissibility as evidence in legal proceedings solely on the grounds that it is in an electronic form or that it does not meet the requirements for qualified electronic signatures. A qualified electronic signature shall have the equivalent legal effect of a handwritten signature for the purposes of Cypriot law. Public sector bodies shall accept qualified electronic signatures created with a qualified certificate issued by a qualified trust service provider included in a trusted list of a Member State, together with, where applicable, the qualified electronic signature validation data. The supervisory body shall provide guidance to public sector bodies on the technical requirements for the verification and acceptance of electronic signatures.

provisions.title: Article 8. Electronic Seals
provisions.content: An electronic seal shall not be denied legal effect and admissibility as evidence in legal proceedings solely on the grounds that it is in electronic form or that it does not meet the requirements for qualified electronic seals. A qualified electronic seal shall enjoy the presumption of integrity of the data and of correctness of the origin of that data to which the qualified electronic seal is linked. Electronic seals may be used to authenticate data issued by legal persons, whether public or private, and shall be accepted by public sector bodies in the Republic of Cyprus when validly created in accordance with the requirements of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 and verified against a trusted list.

provisions.title: Article 9. Electronic Time Stamps
provisions.content: An electronic time stamp shall not be denied legal effect and admissibility as evidence in legal proceedings solely on the grounds that it is in electronic form or that it does not meet the requirements for a qualified electronic time stamp. A qualified electronic time stamp shall enjoy the presumption of the accuracy of the date and the time it indicates and the integrity of the data to which the date and time are bound. Public sector bodies shall accept qualified electronic time stamps issued by qualified trust service providers included in a trusted list for the purposes of establishing the date and time of electronic records in administrative and legal proceedings.

provisions.title: Article 10. Electronic Registered Delivery Services
provisions.content: Data sent and received using a qualified electronic registered delivery service shall enjoy the presumption of the integrity of the data, the sending of that data by the identified sender, its receipt by the identified addressee, and the accuracy of the date and time of sending and receipt indicated by the qualified electronic registered delivery service. Public sector bodies in the Republic of Cyprus shall accept qualified electronic registered delivery services for the purpose of delivering notifications and other official communications where the law requires or permits electronic delivery, provided that the service is provided by a qualified trust service provider included in a trusted list.

provisions.title: Article 11. Website Authentication
provisions.content: A qualified certificate for website authentication issued by a qualified trust service provider shall certify the website's domain name and identify the legal person to which the certificate has been issued. Browsers and other software used by public sector bodies shall recognise and display qualified certificates for website authentication in a manner that clearly distinguishes them from other certificates. The supervisory body may issue technical guidelines specifying requirements for the display and verification of qualified certificates for website authentication in systems used within the public sector. Trust service providers issuing qualified certificates for website authentication shall comply with the technical standards specified by the European Commission.

provisions.title: Article 12. Liability of Trust Service Providers
provisions.content: Trust service providers shall be liable for damages caused intentionally or negligently to any natural or legal person due to a failure to comply with the obligations under this Law and Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The burden of proving intent or negligence of a non-qualified trust service provider shall rest with the natural or legal person claiming damage. A qualified trust service provider shall be presumed to have acted intentionally or negligently unless the qualified trust service provider proves that the damage occurred without intent or negligence. Providers may limit their liability contractually in relation to specific categories of use, provided that such limitations are clearly communicated and do not exclude liability for wilful misconduct.

provisions.title: Article 13. Security Requirements
provisions.content: Trust service providers shall take appropriate technical and organisational measures to manage the risks posed to the security of the trust services they provide. Having regard to the state of the art, these measures shall ensure that the level of security is commensurate with the degree of risk. In particular, measures to prevent and minimise the impact of security incidents and to inform interested parties of the adverse effects of any security incidents shall be implemented. Trust service providers shall notify the supervisory body without undue delay but in any case within twenty-four hours after having become aware of any breach of security or loss of integrity that has a significant impact on the trust service provided or on the personal data maintained therein.

provisions.title: Article 14. International Aspects
provisions.content: The Republic of Cyprus shall recognise trust services provided by trust service providers established in third countries as equivalent to qualified trust services provided by qualified trust service providers established in the Union where the trust services originating from the third country are recognised under an agreement concluded between the Union and the third country concerned. The supervisory body shall provide information to the European Commission on any bilateral arrangements concluded with supervisory bodies of third countries, and shall cooperate with other Member State supervisory bodies in the assessment of the equivalence of third-country trust services.

provisions.title: Article 15. Supervision and Enforcement
provisions.content: The supervisory body shall carry out ex-post supervision of non-qualified trust service providers operating in the Republic of Cyprus, and shall have the power to investigate compliance with applicable requirements. For qualified trust service providers, supervision shall be both ex-ante and ex-post. The supervisory body may require trust service providers to provide all information necessary to assess compliance, may conduct on-site inspections, and may take corrective action where deficiencies are identified. Administrative sanctions applicable to trust service providers that fail to comply with this Law or Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 shall be proportionate and dissuasive and shall be determined taking account of the severity of the breach and the potential harm caused.

provisions.title: Article 16. Interoperability and Standards
provisions.content: The supervisory body shall promote the adoption of standards and technical specifications relevant to trust services, including standards specified by the European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA) and standards developed by European and international standardisation bodies. Public sector bodies procuring trust services shall specify requirements in accordance with applicable standards and shall accept solutions that conform to referenced standards from any qualified trust service provider included in a trusted list. The supervisory body shall participate in cross-border interoperability testing and shall cooperate with the European Commission and other Member States in the development of technical frameworks for the mutual recognition of trust services.

definitions.definition: The process of using person identification data in electronic form uniquely representing either a natural or legal person, or a natural person representing a legal person.

An electronic service normally provided for remuneration which consists of the creation, verification, and validation of electronic signatures, seals, time stamps, electronic registered delivery services, website authentication, or related certificates.
A natural or legal person who provides one or more trust services either as a qualified or as a non-qualified trust service provider.
Data in electronic form which is attached to or logically associated with other data in electronic form and which is used by the signatory to sign.
